output.summary: Implemented accessibility improvements for MasonryCards block: added alt text attribute and control to ImageCard for screen reader support, added ARIA list semantics (role='list' on grid container, role='listitem' on cards) to improve screen reader comprehension of card collections.

filesModified.change: Added 'alt' attribute (type: string, default: empty string)
filesModified.reason: A11Y-001: Provides block-level control for image alt text, allowing authors to override media library alt or explicitly mark images as decorative

filesModified.change: Added TextControl import, extracted alt from attributes, added TextControl in Image PanelBody for alt text input, added role='listitem' to useBlockProps
filesModified.reason: A11Y-001: Provides UI for authors to set custom alt text. A11Y-002: Marks card as list item for screen readers

filesModified.change: Extracted $alt from attributes, conditionally added alt to wp_get_attachment_image attrs array when non-empty, added role='listitem' to wrapper div
filesModified.reason: A11Y-001: Uses custom alt text when provided, falls back to media library alt when empty. A11Y-002: Matches editor ARIA semantics on frontend
A11Y-002: Marks text card as list item for screen readers
A11Y-002: Matches editor ARIA semantics on frontend

filesModified.change: Added role='list' to useInnerBlocksProps first argument
filesModified.reason: A11Y-002: Establishes grid container as semantic list for screen readers

filesModified.change: Added role='list' to grid container div
filesModified.reason: A11Y-002: Matches editor ARIA semantics on frontend

attributesAdded.description: Custom alt text for the image. When empty, falls back to the media library alt text. Allows authors to provide context-specific alt text or explicitly mark images as decorative.
attributesAdded.uiLocation: InspectorControls > Image PanelBody > TextControl below MediaUploadPanel

ariaRolesAdded.purpose: Establishes the card grid as a semantic list collection for screen readers
Marks text card as an item within the list collection
Marks image card as an item within the list collection

syncStatus.details: All ARIA role additions and alt text implementation synchronized between TSX and PHP files. Editor and frontend produce identical ARIA structure.

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (asset size limits) - pre-existing, not related to changes
ESLint warnings and errors in other blocks - pre-existing, not related to MasonryCards changes
buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with 'webpack compiled with 3 warnings'. All warnings are pre-existing performance recommendations. TypeScript compilation passed without errors.

fixesApplied.issue: Image cards have no alt text attribute or override mechanism
fixesApplied.solution: Added 'alt' string attribute to ImageCard block.json, TextControl in ImageCard.tsx sidebar for author input, conditional alt passing to wp_get_attachment_image in ImageCard.php
fixesApplied.verification: Authors can now set custom alt text via sidebar control. When provided, custom alt overrides media library alt. When empty, media library alt is used (or alt='' if media has no alt).

fixesApplied.issue: Grid container lacks semantic role for card collection
fixesApplied.solution: Added role='list' to grid container in MasonryCards.tsx and MasonryCards.php. Added role='listitem' to card wrappers in TextCard.tsx, TextCard.php, ImageCard.tsx, and ImageCard.php
fixesApplied.verification: Screen readers will announce 'list of N items' when encountering the card grid, providing context about the collection structure.

before: Image cards had no block-level alt text control - relied solely on media library alt
Grid was a presentational div with no semantic meaning for screen readers
Cards were divs without any role indication
Screen readers could not identify the card collection as a related group
after: Authors can set custom alt text per image card instance via sidebar control
Custom alt text overrides media library alt when provided
Empty alt attribute can be intentionally used for decorative images
Grid has role='list' establishing it as a semantic collection
Each card has role='listitem' marking it as part of the collection
Screen readers announce 'list of N items' providing collection context
List semantics achieved via ARIA roles without changing CSS Grid structure

designDecisionsImplemented.decision: Use role='list'/role='listitem' ARIA pattern instead of native <ul>/<li> elements
designDecisionsImplemented.rationale: Preserves existing CSS Grid layout structure and nth-child selectors for masonry pattern. Native list elements would require wrapping grid in <ul> and each card in <li>, adding extra markup and potentially breaking layout.
designDecisionsImplemented.implementation: Added role='list' to grid container div, role='listitem' to card wrapper divs in both TSX and PHP

designDecisionsImplemented.decision: Do NOT use ARIA grid pattern (role='grid') for card layout
designDecisionsImplemented.rationale: ARIA grid pattern is for interactive grids requiring arrow-key navigation (data tables, spreadsheets). MasonryCards is a presentational layout of static content. List pattern is semantically correct for content collections.

designDecisionsImplemented.decision: Provide alt text mechanism, do NOT auto-hide decorative images with aria-hidden
designDecisionsImplemented.rationale: Adding aria-hidden='true' to image-only cards would change perceived card count and confuse users. Instead, provide alt text control and trust authors to use it appropriately. Empty alt='' is valid for decorative images.
designDecisionsImplemented.implementation: Added alt attribute and TextControl with help text explaining decorative use case

manualTesting: Verify alt text control appears in ImageCard sidebar under Image panel
Test custom alt text: set custom value, save, check frontend HTML has correct alt attribute
Test empty alt: leave alt field empty, verify media library alt is used (or alt='' if media has none)
Verify role='list' on grid container in rendered HTML
Verify role='listitem' on each card wrapper in rendered HTML
Test with screen reader: confirm grid announced as 'list of N items'
Verify masonry layout still works (nth-child selectors not affected by role attributes)
screenReaderTesting: Use NVDA/JAWS on Windows or VoiceOver on macOS
Confirm announcement: 'list of [N] items' or similar
Tab through cards, confirm each announced as 'list item'
Verify image alt text is announced when present
Verify decorative images (empty alt) are not announced

testPage.note: Test page already updated in previous task with comprehensive variations. No additional test page changes needed for accessibility features.
